title: Bicameralism & Rajya Sabha
lead: The ==Council of States== signifies federal bicameralism (Arts 79–80). Envisaged under the ==GoI Act 1919== (Montagu–Chelmsford); the 1935 vision of a continuous second house never materialised — the Constituent Assembly had ==little prior guidance==.

items.title: Why a Second Chamber
bullets: Check on ==hasty, ill-conceived legislation==
Shares the Lower House's burden
Enables greater ==executive accountability==
Debates wide public issues otherwise unheard
==Guardian of state interests== in a federal polity

items.title: Roles
bullets: ==Revision==: Income Tax (Amendment) Bill 1961, POTA 2002, Delimitation Bill 2002
==Federal==: members elected by state MLAs; All India Services (Art 312); Art 249; approving Emergency when LS is dissolved
==Deliberative==: 12 nominated members (literature, science, art, social service); slower composition change
==Continuity==: never dissolves; ==1/3 retire every 2 years==; pending RS bills don't lapse
No role in ==government formation==; effectively small (250 vs 550)
==Accountability==: 8 of 24 DRSCs; ministers from the RS; all restrictive motions available

items.label: maximum: state/UT representatives (indirectly elected) + presidential nominees
minimum age; elector anywhere in India ([[Kuldip Nayar (2006)]])

items: ==Disruptions==: 2016 — Finance and Appropriation Bills delayed by protests; 2025 uproar over an Opposition leader's remarks.
==Limited powers==: no no-confidence motions; ==Money Bill route circumvents== the RS.
==Solutions==: reinstate domicile; more equal state representation; local-body representation (==Punchhi==); free votes via ADL reform; amend ==Art 110== to allow Money Bill review.

sections.title: RS Elections — Cross-Voting & the Open Ballot

blocks.text: Elections in 10+ states saw ==cross-voting, resort politics, strategic ballot invalidation and inducement allegations== — tension between ==proportional party representation, legislator autonomy and electoral purity==. [[Kuldip Nayar (2006)]] upheld the ==open ballot== and removal of domicile; [[Shailesh Manubhai Parmar (2018)]] struck down ==NOTA== in RS elections.

items.title: The Problems
bullets: ==Cross-voting persists==: Himachal 2024 (34–34 tie, ==won by lot==); March 2026 (Bihar, Odisha, Haryana objections); Jharkhand June 2026
==Open ballot dilemma==: transparency to the party ≠ transparency to the public; enables ==surveillance and retaliation==
==Tenth Schedule gap==: an RS-election cross-vote is ==not an Assembly proceeding== → no automatic disqualification
==Money power==: small electoral college gives individual MLAs ==disproportionate bargaining power==; resorts, abstentions, delayed probes
==High-command nominations==: financiers, celebrities, coalition currency — little internal democracy
==‘Parachute candidates’== post-domicile-removal dilute the Council-of-States character; frequent ==unopposed returns==; STV complexity → decisive ==invalid ballots==; opaque presidential nominations

items.title: Improvements
bullets: ==Preserve PR-STV== — FPTP would let a state's ruling party monopolise seats
==Restricted open ballot==: disclosure only to the party agent; no photography; ECI observers; videographic records; ==expedited review of ballot disputes==
==Democratise nominations==: disclose process, consultation, and the candidate's ==state connection==
==‘State Representation Statement’== instead of a rigid domicile bar
Transparent presidential nominations via a ==non-partisan search committee==
Long-term: ==degressive proportionality== — guaranteed minimum per state, slower growth for the largest
Scrutiny for unopposed returns: affidavits, objections window, public statement of priorities

blocks.text: Rajya Sabha elections will serve federal democracy only when party nominations become democratic, State representation becomes meaningful and bribery becomes swift, certain and politically costly.

sections.title: RS · House of Lords · US Senate

head: Feature
Rajya Sabha
House of Lords
US Senate
rows: CoM responsible ==only to LS==; no no-confidence
Strong checks; ==impeachment trials==; confirms judges, ambassadors, Cabinet; ratifies treaties (2/3)
==Art 249==, Art 312, VP removal; judges' removal; President's impeachment
Revision and expert debate (highest court role ==abolished 2009==)

blocks.text: The Rajya Sabha is not a speed breaker — it is a constitutional conscience: a chamber of second thought where law is refined, not rushed; and diversity is represented, not diluted.
